prevalence.source: ICMR National Health Profile & IHS India
prevalence.trend: increasing due to urbanization and stress

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: Low; no routine national screening, primarily opportunistic in urban clinics
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 25% overall - low in rural areas due to access barriers

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: ICHD-3 with clinical diagnosis in resource-limited settings
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Acute: NSAIDs, limited triptan access; Prophylaxis: propranolol, amitriptyline; Ayurvedic: Shirolepa, Nasya
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Underdiagnosis, limited specialist access, expensive triptans, cultural stigma viewing headaches as normal
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Growing awareness, affordable generic NSAIDs, Ayurvedic alternatives integrated into care
